content: On June 15, 1998, the Atlanta Braves celebrated a significant milestone as they faced the San Francisco Giants. In this game, Braves pitcher Greg Maddux achieved his 200th career win, solidifying his status as one of the premier pitchers of his generation. The victory not only highlighted Maddux's exceptional skill but also underscored the Braves' dominance in the late '90s, as they were in the midst of a remarkable streak of division titles.
